Off Calendar Dec-07-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: ATTY. CLINT J. BRAYTON APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. S. FEY EPLING APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS GLAXOSMITH, ROCKY ROMANO;

After hearing, tentative ruling was adopted. Defendant Glaxosmithkline, Rocky Romano's motion to compel attendance at deposition is moot in light of the Court's ruling as to Plaintiff's motion for a protective order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

For Protective Order; Memorandum Of P/A In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Dec-07-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: ATTY. CLINT J. BRAYTON APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. S. FEY EPLING APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS GLAXOSMITH, ROCKY ROMANO;

After hearing, tentative ruling was adopted. Plaintiff Kathy Mason's motion for a protective order is Granted in part. While counsel for defendant may conduct some inquiry relating to matters referred to in the records, such inquiry shall relate to the emotional distress claims asserted by plaintiff and whether any conditions, events, or conduct caused or contributed to any damages. The identity of any third parties is irrelevant to the subject matter of the action and implicates the right to privacy. The nature of any relationships with third parties is only relevant as it may relate to plaintiff's claim of damages. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to resuming the deposition at a mutually convenient time. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

MOTION TO COMPEL MENTAL EXAMINATION
Off Calendar Dec-07-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: ATTY. CLINT J. BRAYTON APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. S. FEY EPLING APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS GLAXOSMITH, ROCKY ROMANO;

After hearing, the tentative ruling was adopted. Defendant Glaxosmithkline, Rocky Romano's motion to compel a mental examination Granted subject to defendant providing further information to the Court as to the manner, conditions, scope and nature of the examination. The examination may not encompass the sexual history of the plaintiff. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Off Calendar Aug-21-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Patrick J. Suter for plaintiff and moving party Kathy Mason
S. Fey Epling for defendant and opposing party Glaxosmithline and Rocky Romano

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Kathy Mason's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories and request for production of documents
Form interrogatories
200.3, 200.4, 201.1, 201.4, 204.3, 204.5 - 204.7, 207.1, 207.2, 208.1, 211.1: Granted. Any reservation of the right to amend the response is ordered stricken. The response must be full and complete at the time it is made. If there is a need to amend, it must be made in compliance with CCP Section 2030.310. Defendant is ordered to serve supplemental responses within 15 days of notice of this order. The answers do not fully comply with CCP Section 2030.230 as it is implied that other responsive documents exist that have yet to be identified.
Request for production of documents: defendant GSK
1 - 12: Granted. Defendant is ordered to serve a supplemental response within 15 days of notice of this order that complies with CCP Section 2031.280(a).
6, 7, 9, 10: Granted. Defendant is ordered to provide responsive documents not subject to the limitations set forth in the supplemental responses.
Request for production of documents: defendant Romano
16 - 20: Granted. Defendant is ordered to serve a supplemental response within 15 days of notice of this order that complies with CCP Section 2031.280(a). COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Off Calendar May-18-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
S. Fey Epling for Defendant and Moving Party Glaxosmithkline, Rocky Romano
Cael Davis for Plaintiff and Opposing Party Kathy Mason

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Glaxosmithkline, Rocky Romano's motion to compel plaintiff to provide authorization to release records. The motion is moot in light of the Court's ruling as to plaintiff's motion to quash medical records subpoenas. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Medical Records Subpoenas
Off Calendar May-18-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Cael Davis for Plaintiff and Moving Party Kathy Mason
S. Fey Epling for Defendant and Opposing Party Glaxosmithkline, Rocky Romano

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Kahty Mason's motion to quash is denied; however, the Court will modify the subpoenas. As to requests 1 - 4 and 9, the subpoenas are modified to only require production of records from 1998 to the present. As to requests 5 - 8, 10 - 13, the subpoenas are modified to only require production of records from 1998 to the present relating to plaintiff's back and any other orthopedic conditions. The parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding the stipulated protective order previously proposed by counsel for defendant. Comm. Bruce E. Chan.
